title: German Field Radio in the Fortified Cellar
description: In the cleared German cellar on Rue du Moulin, a compact German field radio — a compact German field communications set — sits on a shelf with its handset off the hook. It is warm to the touch. A slip of paper beside it carries a six-digit grid reference in pencil — coordinates that, when checked against the artillery map, correspond precisely to the gap in coverage created by the falsified fire tables. The grid reference is written in a hand that is not German.
